title: acacia deltoidea subsp. ampla
intro: Acacia deltoidea subsp. ampla, also known as the Muka or Damaru, is a species of tree in the pea family Fabaceae, native to tropical Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Acacia deltoidea subsp. ampla include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to support the immune system and reduce inflammation. The herbal preparations of this plant include decoctions, infusions, and powders, which can be consumed as teas, capsules, or added to food products.
